package: Goal 3 tensor-network / holographic-code extension certificate index
scope: Capstone package for existing Goal 3 phases, with no new search phase added.

status_legend.exact_finite_certificate: A concrete finite construction or proof-obligation package verified by exact stabilizer, min-cut, reconstruction, and channel diagnostics.
status_legend.exact_exhaustive_bounded_search: All objects in a stated finite bounded family are enumerated and verified exactly.
status_legend.exploratory_bounded_evidence: Earlier bounded searches that are exact for searched records but not exhaustive for a broad mathematical family.
status_legend.conjectural_interpretation: A broader reading suggested by the finite evidence, not certified as a theorem.

objects.tensor_network_layer: Finite stabilizer tensor-network / holographic-code-like constructions with declared boundary regions and finite min-cut skeletons.
objects.source_code_family: Graph/CWS stabilizer source-code pairs, with the final representative using source ordinals [21, 27].
objects.outer_layer: Y-axis interface-star outer layer with an alternating Clifford/MERA-like variant.
objects.boundary_region: A finite set of physical qubits named by the region grammar; the final representative is root_shell_plus_edge_0_minus_q139 with q0 and q3 added.

diagnostics.entropy_visible: Exact stabilizer subsystem entropy for the boundary region.
diagnostics.min_cut_visible: Exact finite graph min-cut values over declared capacity profiles.
diagnostics.reconstruction_visible: Exact region algebra and logical reconstruction diagnostics.
diagnostics.channel_visible: Exact erasure-correctability, survivor fixed-point, and channel-semantics diagnostics.

major_claims.title: Final finite holographic-cousin witness
major_claims.claim: There exists a certified finite tensor-network/holographic-code-like stabilizer pair whose boundary-region entropy and min-cut diagnostics agree while reconstruction-visible and channel-visible diagnostics differ.
major_claims.scope: Declared Phase 38/39 tensor-network layer and final representative witness.

major_claims.title: Phase 40 proof-obligation audit
major_claims.claim: Six proof obligations are satisfied: entropy-visible agreement, exact capacity-sensitive min-cut evidence, reconstruction-visible split, channel/erasure split, Phase 38 family support, and Phase 39 local robustness.

major_claims.title: q139 support-scale strict-hit family
major_claims.claim: The declared Phase 38 search finds 25 admissible entropy/min-cut/operator hits, including 15 channel-visible hits and 10 operator-only hits.
major_claims.scope: Three graph/CWS source pairs, five alternating variants, 110 unique q139 support regions, 1650 candidate support records, and 10 capacity profiles.

major_claims.title: Representative local-neighborhood robustness
major_claims.claim: The representative is not a one-record accident: 34 of 52 local-neighborhood records remain strict channel-visible hits.
major_claims.scope: Same-leaf offset alternatives, single-qubit deletions, and bounded local single additions around the representative.

major_claims.title: Earlier tensor-network family exploration
major_claims.claim: Earlier phases explored CSS/stabilizer tensor-network seeds, graph/CWS ring-spoke toys, Clifford/MERA-like circuits, perfect/HaPPY-like blocks, and chain/ring/tree tiling toy networks with exact diagnostics inside stated bounds.
major_claims.note: Representative commands are listed for family landmarks; the evidence is exact for searched records but not a theorem over all such tensor-network families.

major_claims.title: Conjectural interpretation
major_claims.claim: Matching entropy or finite min-cut summaries need not determine observer-accessible bulk/logical geometry in holographic-code-like finite QEC models.
major_claims.scope: Suggested by the finite exact witness and bounded searches; not certified as a universal theorem.

not_overclaimed: No asymptotic RT theorem is claimed.
No universal holographic-code classification is claimed.
No global minimality theorem is claimed.
No all-axis robustness theorem is claimed.
The exhaustive claims are limited to their declared finite bounded families.

related_goals.goal1: Balanced-bridge CSS theorem: low-order entropy diagnostics can match while reconstruction/algebra profiles differ.
related_goals.goal2: Finite causal-patch / horizon-code toy cosmology: named patch entropy and shared-horizon data can match while observer reconstruction, erasure semantics, and channel behavior differ.
related_goals.goal3: Tensor-network / holographic-code-like extension: entropy/min-cut-visible geometry can agree while reconstruction-visible and channel-visible geometry split.
